q:0: Draft a legally compliant performance improvement plan (PIP) and termination checklist for an underperforming salaried customer-support employee in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India, at a 60-person tech company. Include scripts, a 30/60/90-day timeline, and documentation templates for meetings, warnings, and final separation.

q:1: Create an IP risk checklist and filing roadmap (patents, trademarks, trade secrets) for a new product: “MantaTest,” an open‑source GPL C# OOP neural‑network framework (http://www.mantatest.com/). Include priority deadlines, steps for US first filing/PCT, trademark clearance/filing, trade secret controls, and a basic IP portfolio tracker template.

q:2: Build a step-by-step checklist (with required forms and evidence) to apply for VA health care and verify eligibility, including how to use VA.gov and what to do if denied.

q:3: Given this job description for a Records & Information Management role (ICRM-related) from http://www.gamerevolution.com/oldsite/games/dreamcast/action/maken_x.htm, rewrite my resume summary and bullets for ATS and role priorities. My background: 5 years records management, retention schedules, eDiscovery, SharePoint, compliance, process improvement. Note key hiring-manager priorities.

q:4: Make a country-by-country briefing comparing how India, Japan, and Brazil frame the UN Security Council reform debate, with key cultural/historical context and recent diplomacy timeline.

q:5: Create a triage decision tree for my dog’s vomiting and diarrhea (include red flags, at-home steps, and when to seek emergency care vs schedule a vet visit).

q:6: Can you help me map my anxiety triggers by asking me a few questions, then summarize patterns and give a personalized 7-day coping plan with daily check-ins?

q:7: Create a HACCP-style risk assessment for my homemade refrigerated chicken salad. Ingredients: cooked diced chicken breast, mayonnaise, celery, red onion, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, salt, pepper. Process: cook chicken to 74°C/165°F, cool, dice, mix with ingredients, portion into sealed containers, refrigerate, serve. Identify hazards, critical control points, critical limits, monitoring/corrective actions, and estimate safe shelf life at ≤4°C/40°F.

q:8: Review my SaaS services agreement for AcmeCloud (B2B subscription software) with Customer XYZ (US-based). Provide prioritized redlines and a negotiation plan (fallback positions, tradeoffs, and email wording) to reduce liability and tighten payment and termination terms. Assume current contract includes broad indemnity, uncapped damages, net-60, and vague termination.

q:9: Create a 90-day retention plan for our 25-person startup, including manager actions, stay-interview questions, and metrics to track voluntary turnover monthly.

q:10: Set up a Zapier automation: new Typeform lead → enrich with Clearbit → create/update HubSpot contact + deal → post to Slack, and alert me if enrichment fails.

q:11: I’m a Sales Operations Manager at a 200-person B2B SaaS company using Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, and Google Sheets. Analyze my weekly tasks (lead routing, pipeline hygiene, forecast reporting, territory changes, deal desk approvals, dashboard upkeep, enablement, renewals handoffs) and bundle into AI-assisted workflows with automations, suggested tools, time-saved estimates, and a 30/60/90-day rollout plan.

q:12: Design a Make.com scenario that auto-replies to new Gmail emails using a Google Sheets FAQ lookup, includes error handling, and outputs a step-by-step module blueprint.

q:13: Audit February 2026 cross-channel marketing spend and ROI for Acme D2C (Google Search/Shopping, Meta, TikTok, email, affiliates). Provide budget reallocation, 30-day optimization plan with KPI targets, team owners, and an analytics tracking checklist (UTMs, GA4, pixels/CAPI, offline conversions, attribution).

q:14: Find current deals on premium project-management tools under $10/month (e.g., Notion, Asana alternatives) and list the best offers with links and renewal prices.

q:15: Create a shareable daily “Visitor Sign-in” form with required fields, a liability acknowledgment checkbox, and an auto-generated timestamp; include a simple admin review checklist.

q:16: I’m moving into a small apartment—give me a 30‑minute, low-cost plan (with materials list) to organize my kitchen and reduce food waste using simple DIY hacks.

q:17: Turn my raw meeting notes (below) from a 1-hour project sync (Mar 6, 2026, 10:00–11:00 AM ET; attendees: Alex Chen, Priya Patel, Jordan Lee) into polished minutes with: agenda sections, key discussion points, decisions, action items (owner + due date), parking lot, and a 3–5 sentence email-ready recap. Notes: [PASTE NOTES HERE].

q:18: I’m meeting Sarah Chen (VP Partnerships at a B2B SaaS procurement platform) tomorrow to discuss a potential integration/reseller deal. Using professional insights from this video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B8TOz25ctGw, draft a 30‑minute tailored meeting agenda, 6 rapport starters, and 5 likely objections with concise responses.

q:19: Given this job description for an ATS-screened role at ImproveNet (radiodata.com) in the home appliances market, identify what the company and hiring manager likely prioritize, then rewrite my current resume bullet points to match the required skills/keywords. Assume I’m a mid-level product marketing manager with 5 years’ experience in consumer appliances.

q:20: In Figma, build a reusable color + typography token system (light/dark) and show the exact token names plus CSS variable equivalents I can paste into my codebase.

q:21: Create a Figma-ready wireframe (screens + components list) for a mobile inventory system: login, dashboard, scan item, add/edit item, stock adjustments, audit log, and alerts.

q:22: Create a low‑fidelity Figma wireframe for a mobile checkout flow (cart → address → payment → review), with frame list, component labels, and layout specs.

q:23: Design a cohesive set of 12 minimalist space-themed app icons as a single downloadable PNG grid, with consistent stroke/size and transparent background.

q:24: Create PlantUML for a data flow diagram of an online food ordering system (customer, restaurant, payment gateway), including processes, data stores, and labeled data flows.

q:25: Create a 1:1 neon cyberpunk coffee logo image, then save it and give me a shareable link plus downloadable PNG and SVG versions.

q:27: Create 3 ultra-detailed FLUX.1 photoreal cinematic prompts for a rain-soaked neon alley detective scene, with camera/lens, lighting, composition, wardrobe, and negative prompts.

q:28: Convert my Figma design (assume a SaaS dashboard UI with header, sidebar, buttons, form inputs, cards, table, and modal) into a responsive React + TypeScript component library using Tailwind. Include exported SVG assets, folder/file structure, and paste-ready code examples for each component.

q:29: Convert a Figma frame screenshot of a modern landing-page hero (top nav with logo and 4 links, primary CTA button, left-aligned headline/subtext, and right-side image placeholder) into responsive semantic HTML/CSS with matching spacing/colors and hover states. Output a single clean code snippet.

q:30: I have two photos of a cheek rash (photo A taken today, photo B taken 3 days ago). Based only on typical visual changes, estimate likely causes (e.g., contact dermatitis, acne, eczema, infection), list red-flag symptoms needing urgent care, and give a 7-day home-care plan including step-by-step patch-testing for new products.

q:31: Create a step-by-step triage checklist and 2-week treatment plan for a suspected eczema flare (adult), including OTC options, red flags, and when to book dermatology follow-up.

q:32: Create a 2-week nail recovery plan for brittle, peeling nails after gel removal—daily steps, product checklist, and hygiene rules; include a simple progress tracker.

q:33: Using my uploaded face selfie (I’m a 28-year-old with combination skin, mild acne on chin, visible pores on nose, some redness on cheeks), analyze likely skin concerns and create a morning/night skincare checklist. Recommend 3 budget and 3 high-end products tailored to these concerns, with brief usage notes.

q:34: Create a Wonyoungism-style morning-to-night schedule with skincare layering steps (AM/PM), a beginner workout, and 5 daily affirmations tailored to oily, acne-prone skin and school days.

q:35: Create a 7-day weight-loss meal plan (daily calories + macros) and a grocery list using these details: 32-year-old female, 5'6", 170 lb, goal 1 lb/week loss; strength trains 3x/week + 8k steps/day; current diet: oatmeal, chicken/rice, salads, snacks; must-haves: eggs, Greek yogurt, chicken, salmon, quinoa, spinach; lactose-sensitive.

q:36: Using current guidelines and key trials, draft a SOAP note and evidence-based treatment plan for an adult with chronic insomnia, including CBT-I steps and medication options.

q:37: Create an evidence-based differential and stepwise workup for acute hypoxemic respiratory failure with bilateral infiltrates, including ventilation strategies, meds, contraindications, and patient handout points.

q:38: Create a 2-week supplement-and-herbal plan for better sleep and lower stress, including dosages, timing, safety interactions, and a simple shopping list.

q:39: I have a headache, stiff neck, and fever since last night—can you triage me with a red-flag checklist and tell me whether I should go to urgent care or ER today?

q:40: Compare EU GDPR vs Brazil LGPD for my SaaS; produce a compliance checklist, data-processing agreement clauses, and a risk table for cross-border transfers.

q:41: Assume I’m defense counsel in a U.S. federal civil case. Based on an uploaded complaint alleging defamation and consumer-complaint retaliation tied to posts on http://www.mmkhost.com/, identify the strongest Rule 12(b)(6) dismissal arguments and provide a concise motion outline with key supporting case citations.

q:42: Draft a Philippine Rule 65 Petition for Certiorari with urgent prayer for TRO/Writ of Preliminary Injunction assailing an RTC (Branch 45, Quezon City) Order dated 15 Jan 2026 denying respondent defendant Juan Dela Cruz’s Motion to Dismiss a civil collection case for lack of jurisdiction/prescription. Include key jurisprudence, verification/certification, and a list of annexes.

q:43: Find and quote the current NSW law section(s) defining ‘assault’ and ‘common assault’, and list the maximum penalties with the latest consolidation date and official source link.

q:44: Create a 4-week bilingual (EN/ES) learning plan for a practicing lawyer to learn AI basics, with weekly exercises and a checklist mapping key points to the EU AI Act.

q:45: Help me craft a counteroffer email and a negotiation script for a $120k job offer, including 3 anchor options, fallback perks, and a step-by-step prep checklist.

q:46: Check AS/NZS 3000:2018 compliance for relocating a light switch beside a bathroom vanity—IP requirements, RCD rules, and prohibited zones; give a step-by-step checklist.

q:47: Create a step-by-step runbook to launch a small online course in 14 days, with daily tasks, checklist, budget, risk log, and ready-to-copy email/landing-page drafts.

q:48: Turn Palsgraf v. Long Island Railroad into a tight case brief and add a one-page Torts outline section (duty/foreseeability/proximate cause) with exam-spotting tips.

q:49: Given this scenario: I paid $3,000 via Venmo to a freelance contractor to repair my car; they took the money, did shoddy work causing a brake failure and crash injuries, then refused refunds and threatened me. Compare possible tort, contract, and criminal claims; list elements/defenses, remedies, and evidence to gather in a decision table.

q:50: I’m overwhelmed choosing a decision framework for prioritizing my next 6 weeks of work—ask me key questions and produce a tailored framework, steps, and a one-page template.

q:51: Find a free API that returns 7‑day hourly weather forecasts by lat/long, compare 2 options, and give a curl example plus rate-limit/auth details.

q:52: Solve LeetCode “Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum” with step-by-step reasoning and final Python + Java code, including edge cases and complexity.

q:53: Help me identify a real problem I can solve, then turn it into a startup idea using a problem→aggravation→solution framework and an ideal customer profile.

q:54: Explain the optimal approach for Codeforces 1919C “Grouping Increases”: core idea, invariants, step-by-step walkthrough on a sample input, and time/memory complexity.

q:55: Draft a detailed Scope of Work for a fixed-price website redesign, including deliverables, exclusions, acceptance criteria, milestones, roles, and change-control process.

q:56: Draft a 30‑minute user interview plan (screeners, 10 questions, probes, and note‑taking template) to uncover why users abandon onboarding in my app.

q:57: Apply a 4-perspective reframing matrix (Product, Planning, Potential, People) to reduce churn for our B2B SaaS (project management tool, 500 paying SMB accounts, 6% monthly logo churn, main reasons: poor onboarding, missing integrations, slow support). Provide a prioritized action plan for 0–30, 1–3, and 3–6 months.

q:58: Analyze the website http://www.picosecondate.com and list detectable web technologies it uses (frameworks, libraries, analytics, CDN) based on its publicly accessible HTML/CSS/JS source code. Use only evidence from the site’s source (scripts, meta tags, headers if available).

q:59: Using Koray Tuğberk’s Semantic SEO framework, build a topical map and semantic content network for a niche site about fan-subtitled TV/anime series hosting on Gotwoot Network (http://www.alyssaalps.com/). Include an entity graph, internal linking rules, and an optimal publishing sequence.

q:60: Design a GitHub Actions workflow to build/test a Node app on PRs, cache dependencies, and publish a Docker image on tags; include exact YAML and secrets setup.

q:61: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests on PRs, builds and tags a Docker image on main merges, and publishes release notes from conventional commits.

q:62: Analyze the GitHub issue thread at https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/issues/1234. Provide a concise summary of the discussion, highlighting key points raised by participants, any proposed fixes or workarounds, and a short, clear list of recommended next steps (e.g., who should do what, what to test, what to document).

q:63: Review the PR diff for my Node.js/TypeScript Express API (auth + payments) and return a git patch containing full updated files, plus a prioritized list of bugs, security issues (OWASP, secrets, auth), and performance fixes. Assume the PR adds a new /checkout endpoint and refactors middleware; include specific file paths and line-level notes.

q:64: In https://github.com/microsoft/semantic-kernel, where is the Qdrant vector store implemented, and what are the main classes/methods for upsert and similarity search?

q:65: Find 5 well-scoped GitHub issues for a Python contributor (skill level 3/5) I can finish in a weekend, with repo links and why each fits.

q:66: Find the top 10 GitHub repositories for "Rust async runtime" and list each repo with stars, brief description, and link.

q:67: Scrape Reddit posts and comments about the Steam Deck OLED vs ROG Ally, filter out promo/spam, and summarize the top pros/cons and recurring issues.

q:68: Give only the exact ADB commands to: list devices, connect over TCP/IP to 192.168.1.50:5555, install app.apk, and view filtered logcat for tag "MyApp".

q:69: In the GitHub repo https://github.com/psf/requests, identify the module/function(s) where HTTP retry logic is implemented (e.g., adapters/urllib3 integration). Then outline the exact code edits to add exponential backoff with jitter (which files, what new parameters/defaults, and how to compute sleep time).

q:80: Given a patient’s whole-genome FASTA and VCF (assume adult male with unexplained cardiomyopathy and family history of sudden cardiac death), perform variant calling/QC, annotate variants (ClinVar/gnomAD/OMIM), flag ACMG pathogenic/likely pathogenic findings, and produce a structured clinical-style report with literature references.

q:81: Create a step-by-step research plan and annotated bibliography (10 peer-reviewed sources) for a thesis on algorithmic bias in hiring, including search strings and a week-by-week timeline.

q:82: Using Dimensions evidence, summarize 2020–2025 studies on microplastics’ effects on human gut microbiome, with key findings, top cited papers, and formatted citations.

q:83: Solve this implicit differentiation problem and verify numerically with code: for \(x^2y+\sin(xy)=1\), find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) at \((x,y)=(1,0)\). Explain in English and Spanish with LaTeX.

q:84: Create a prioritized customer experience (CX) transformation roadmap for a mid-size B2C subscription SaaS (50k customers, 12% churn, CSAT 78). Map the end-to-end journey (awareness→onboarding→support→renewal), define KPIs/VoC (NPS, CSAT, CES, churn, retention, FCR), estimate ROI per initiative, and draft a 90-day action plan.

q:85: Draft a legally compliant U.S. employee handbook for a remote‑first tech company (50 employees) headquartered in Texas with staff in CA, NY, FL, IL, and WA. Include core policies plus state‑specific addenda, PTO/leave, harassment prevention, discipline/termination, equipment/security, and an implementation rollout checklist.

q:86: Using my uploaded tomato plant photos (link: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/10/My_captures.jpg), diagnose what’s harming them (pests vs disease vs nutrient deficiency) and give an organic treatment plan plus a 4-week care schedule. Assume I’m a home gardener in USDA Zone 7b with in-ground tomatoes, watered 3x/week.

q:87: Create a complete Overleaf-ready IEEE-style LaTeX project (10-page lab report) with full folder/.tex structure, main.tex, sections (Abstract–Conclusion), sample figures/tables using https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/fb/A_Lake_For_All.jpg, bibliography (.bib + citations), appendices, and reusable macros. Include placeholder text and build instructions.

q:88: Create a GeneXus procedure and data model to import a CSV into a SQL Server table with validation, error log table, and transaction rollback.

q:89: From Boericke + Kent repertory, compile key rubrics and remedy indications for acute gastritis (burning epigastric pain, nausea after food); cite sources, no diagnosis.

q:110: Write 6 photoreal Midjourney v6.1 prompts for a luxury smartwatch lifestyle ad (NYC), diverse models, golden-hour realism, brand-safe styling, lens/lighting details, plus negatives and parameters.

q:111: Create 10 Midjourney watercolor prompts for Adobe Stock about “cozy rainy street café”, each with stock-friendly composition, clean negatives, and varied camera angles/lighting.

q:112: Upgrade my rough prompt into 3 Midjourney + 3 FLUX-ready variants (with aspect ratio, lighting, lens, style tags, and negative terms): “cat chef serving winter hot cocoa ad”.

q:113: Create one Midjourney prompt for a trending stock photo about “remote healthcare telemedicine,” photorealistic, high contrast, golden ratio composition, default 16:9.

q:114: Make a next-level Midjourney prompt: cyberpunk samurai doing a beer advertisement in neon Tokyo rain, cinematic photoreal+anime mix, include parameters (ar, stylize, quality, seed).

q:115: Create one Midjourney v7 RAW prompt for a moody cinematic photo of a lone lighthouse in a storm; include camera/lens, lighting, composition, and v7 parameters.

q:116: Create 5 photorealistic Midjourney prompts for a 1930s female influencer on an alien planet, with camera/lens, lighting, aspect ratio, and --style raw/--v parameters.

q:117: Create 5 Midjourney V6 portrait photo prompts for a moody cinematic close-up of a jazz singer, including lens, lighting setup, skin texture, and negative prompts.

q:118: Using this reference image https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/fb/21_the_figure_on_the_right_has_disappeared%2C_but_one_foot_remains.jpg (stone sculpture fragments with one foot visible), write one Midjourney v6.1 prompt matching the subject and lighting but changing the setting to rainy neon Tokyo at night; include --ar and --stylize.

q:119: Turn this short script about Effective Altruism (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9vehER01Fdg) into a single Midjourney v6.1/v7 cinematic image prompt (not a storyboard). Use a dramatic cave setting inspired by https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/86/Between_a_Rock_and_a_Light_Place_%2814823509169%29.jpg. Include lens, lighting, camera angle, color grade, and MJ parameters.

q:120: Audit my dating app profile: I’m Alex, 29, Seattle; bio: “Coffee, hikes, and sarcasm. Here for something real.” Review 6 photos incl. https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/10/My_captures.jpg and a charity talk clip https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PHgdXWZLs9g. Rewrite bio, reorder photos, give prioritized changes, and 3 opening messages for top matches.

q:121: Audit my Tinder profile for a 29-year-old straight man in Seattle who likes hiking and anime; rewrite my bio and 3 prompts, choose my best 5 photos from the ones I’ll upload plus this mountain shot https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/10/My_captures.jpg, and write 3 openers matching a witty, upbeat vibe.

q:122: I’m a 28-year-old guy in Seattle seeking a witty, kind Tinder vibe. Given my current bio and six photos (including a mountain photo: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/10/My_captures.jpg), rewrite my bio, recommend the best photo order, and suggest 3 opening messages that fit my tone.

q:123: Using Robert Greene’s “Seduction,” design a 7-day, step-by-step seduction plan for a coworker crush (boundaries-safe), including daily actions, sample lines, and escalation checkpoints.

q:124: Create a step-by-step text-message plan (with exact lines and timing) to re-engage a woman who went cold after a great first date, without seeming needy.

q:125: Create 6 irresistible texts for a Bumble match: warm opener + 4-message arc + close. Topic: museums/coffee shops; include hooks, playful tension, and one date invite.

q:126: Given a chat screenshot between me (28M) and a woman I’m dating (26F) where she’s being flaky and testing boundaries, analyze the messages and suggest a confident, respectful plan: frame, boundaries, when to pull back, and 5 exact texts to reassert self-respect without chasing or manipulative “red‑pill” dominance.

q:127: Create a step-by-step game plan for approaching women at a bar tonight—openers, body language cues, what to say after, and how to smoothly get her number.

q:128: Create a 14-day plan with daily prompts and exact scripts to calmly discuss our sex life with my wife without triggering defensiveness, plus check-ins and success metrics.

q:129: Build a 12-message, witty-but-explicit dating-app chat script to move from first DM to a consensual NSA hookup; include 3 branches for her responses and a safe-sex close.

q:130: Create a thematically consistent 8-color palette for a coastal coffee brand—include HEX codes plus primary/secondary/accent/neutral roles and accessibility-friendly light/dark background pairings.

q:131: Create a seamless repeating wallpaper pattern: mid-century geometric arches, 4-color palette, include tile dimensions, repeat offset, and print-ready PNG + SVG export settings.

q:132: Help me generate a hero banner image—ask me for theme, on-image text, key objects, color palette, and style—then produce the final image prompt/output.

q:133: Create a modern 6-color palette inspired by Duolingo, include HEX codes and a simple preview image showing the colors side by side.

q:134: Using my uploaded selfie (not the provided bridge image), analyze my complexion, hair, and eye color to recommend a cohesive 12-color wardrobe + makeup palette. Provide each color’s name + HEX, identify best neutrals vs accent colors, and briefly explain the inferred skin undertone match.

q:135: Create 6 image variations from just four words: “neon koi cyberpunk alley,” each with different color palettes and lighting moods; output ready-to-use prompts.
